channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian Racers Paige Raddatz and Joanne Ciconte Start Kyojo Cup Season in Japan
item.description: Australian motor racing talents Paige Raddatz and Joanne Ciconte are set to make their debuts in the 2026 Kyojo Cup, an all-female racing series held at Japan's Fuji Speedway. The opening round will t...

item.title: Kano Governor Abba Yusuf submits nomination form to APC for 2027 governorship election
item.description: Governor Abba Kabir Yusuf of Kano State don officially submit him nomination form to the National Working Committee of the All Progressives Congress (APC) for him to run as the party candidate in the ...

item.title: Reform UK Leads in Local Elections as Labour Suffers Significant Losses
item.description: Early results from the local elections across parts of England indicate a clear lead for Reform UK, who are securing roughly a third of the seats declared so far. Labour, defending the most seats in t...

item.title: Senate to Begin Impeachment Trial of VP Sara Duterte Once Articles Are Transmitted
item.description: Retired Supreme Court Associate Justice Antonio Carpio clarified that under the Philippine Constitution, the Senate is mandated to convene as an impeachment court immediately once the House of Represe...

item.title: PSA Debunks False Claims of Free Electricity and Subsidies for National ID and Vaccine Card Holders
item.description: The Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) has officially debunked viral claims suggesting that holders of the national ID or COVID-19 vaccine cards can avail of free electricity and various government...

item.title: Stellantis launches Morocco centre for dismantling and recycling vehicles in Africa
item.description: Stellantis, the parent company of Peugeot and Jeep, has inaugurated its first vehicle dismantling centre in the Middle East and Africa, located in Casablanca, Morocco. The €1.6 million (R31 million) f...

item.title: Hantavirus Cases Confirmed on MV Hondius Cruise Ship in Atlantic
item.description: A recent hantavirus outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship in the Atlantic has led to multiple fatalities and raised international health concerns. According to the World Health Organization (WHO)...

item.title: Palace Clarifies Franco Mabanta’s Brief Role as Marcos' Social Media Director in 2018
item.description: Malacañang clarified that Franco Mabanta, founder of Peanut Gallery Media Network (PGMN), only served as President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr.’s social media director for about six months in 2018....

item.title: Dagoretti North MP Elachi Plans to Make Public Education Free for All Pupils
item.description: Dagoretti North MP Beatrice Elachi has unveiled plans to abolish fees in all public schools within her constituency, aiming to ease financial burdens for struggling parents. Elachi highlighted that ed...

item.title: CRDB reports Sh206bn profit and plans for continued growth in 2026
item.description: Dar es Salaam – CRDB Bank Plc is showing solid growth prospects for 2026 after posting a first-quarter profit after tax of Sh206.2 billion. According to CRDB Group CEO, Abdulmajid Nsekela, the perform...

item.title: Barack Obama Features in Trailer for Larry David’s Upcoming HBO Sketch Show
item.description: Former U.S. President Barack Obama makes a cameo appearance in the teaser trailer for Larry David’s upcoming HBO sketch comedy series, Life, Larry and the Pursuit of Unhappiness. In the clip, Obama hu...

item.title: OG Anunoby Leaves Game 2 with Possible Hamstring Injury
item.description: During Game 2 of the Eastern Conference playoffs, OG Anunoby left the court early due to a suspected right hamstring injury while the New York Knicks secured a 108-102 victory over the Philadelphia 76...

item.title: Fuel price hikes increase transport and food costs across Tanzania
item.description: Consumers and traders across Tanzania are feeling the pinch as rising fuel prices continue to push up transport costs and retail prices of food. The situation is affecting the whole supply chain, from...

item.title: Kyle Sandilands claims on-air fights with Jackie O were encouraged and monetised by Kiis FM before his sacking
item.description: In a multimillion-dollar legal battle between Kyle Sandilands, Jackie O Henderson, and ARN Media, fresh court documents reveal claims that heated exchanges between the radio hosts were treated as valu...

item.title: Trump sets July 4 deadline for EU trade deal completion amid tariff threats
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced that the European Union has until July 4 to finalise a long-delayed trade agreement, warning that tariffs on European goods could sharply increase if the deal...

item.title: KP’s Bajaur constable found dead following abduction by unidentified men
item.description: In Bajaur district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, a tragic incident occurred where a police constable was abducted on Thursday while heading to his post in Sindh Gara and later found dead on a mountain in Asi...

item.title: Canada Faces Challenges in Meeting Rising Energy Demand Amid Global Shifts
item.description: Global events, particularly the war in Iran, have significantly altered the flow of oil and gas, leading to increased demand for North American energy exports, including petroleum and liquefied natura...

item.title: Lulu Hassan and Rashid Abdalla Debate Modern Relationships on Citizen TV
item.description: During a live segment on Citizen TV, popular TV personalities Lulu Hassan and Rashid Abdalla sparked lively debate after clashing over modern relationship issues. Rashid tried reading a fan's message ...

item.title: 'Tayo sa Wakas' OST Launch and Fan Event Reaches Full Capacity
item.description: The highly anticipated launch of the original soundtrack (OST) for the popular Filipino series 'Tayo sa Wakas' has officially sold out, along with its exclusive fan meet. The event, organized by One M...

item.title: Tanzania and EU Enhance Long-Term Trade and Investment Cooperation
item.description: Tanzania and the European Union (EU) have continued to boost their strong partnership, focusing on trade, investment, and private sector growth. Minister for Industry and Trade, Ms Judith Kapinga, sai...
